title: bromus mollis subsp. lepidus
intro: Bromus mollis subsp. lepidus, also known as soft brome or meadow brome, is a subspecies of perennial grass native to Europe. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects. The health benefits of Bromus mollis subsp. lepidus include soothing respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant's flowers and leaves can be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
